prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye scurvy dog, gather 'round the mast and hark! The sky be blue as the Devil’s own bloomers 'cause the sun, that fiery tyrant, flings his gold across the heavens, and the air—aye, the very breath we suck—grabs hold o’ the sun’s bright booty and scatters the blue light like a tavern wench flingin’ doubloons to a crowd o’ drunken swabs. The blue, bein’ a nimble little sprite, leaps and dances in every direction, whilst the redder hues, dull as a bosun’s wit, plod straight ahead. So when ye cast yer gaze skyward, ‘tis only the blue that bounces into yer peepers, and thus the firmament wears the color of the Caribbean itself. Now quit yer gawpin’ and swab the deck afore I keelhaul ye!
